mission.title: Install and Configure Xregistry on Kubernetes
mission.description: xRegistry is a metadata registry for discovering, validating, and versioning resources in distributed systems. Installing it allows you to manage and interact with registries effectively using the provided server and CLI tools.

steps.title: Add the xRegistry Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official xRegistry Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration. This allows you to install xRegistry using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add xregistry https://xregistry.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install xRegistry using Helm
steps.description:
Install the xRegistry server with a specified release name and create a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install xregistry xregistry/xregistry --namespace xregistry --create-namespace --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the xRegistry pods are running successfully in the xregistry namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the xRegistry deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/xregistry --namespace xregistry --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the xRegistry service
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the xRegistry service locally for testing.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/xregistry --namespace xregistry 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the xRegistry Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall xregistry --namespace xregistry
```

uninstall.title: Clean up resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining custom resource definitions (CRDs) related to xRegistry.
```bash
kubectl delete crd xregistries.xregistry.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the xRegistry namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the xRegistry namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace xregistry
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data from xRegistry.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace xregistry -o yaml > xregistry-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the xRegistry Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade xRegistry
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the xRegistry release to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade xregistry xregistry/xregistry --namespace xregistry --version 0.1.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the xRegistry pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the xRegistry pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the xRegistry service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that might indicate what is going wrong.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with configuration, verify that the resource limits and requests are set correctly.
```bash
kubectl get deployment xregistry --namespace xregistry -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of xRegistry will have the pods running without errors and accessible via the specified port. You should be able to interact with the xRegistry service using the CLI or HTTP requests.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add xregistry https://xregistry.io/charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
